eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 4 年级

chrome.sub: A student thought old posts would just disappear. They didn't. Crack four locks to learn why the internet remembers.
chrome.briefP: Months ago, a student posted photos, sent messages, and used a picture that was not theirs. Today they found all of it still online. Read the clues, then open all four locks. Each lock has a reason that shows why the answer is right.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You learned that your online record can last a long time, how to use good manners in messages, how to give a creator credit, and how to be an upstander who stops cyberbullying. Great detective work!

clues.title: Why is it still there?
clues.body: When you post online, a copy can stay for a long time. Even if you delete it, others may have saved or shared it. This lasting trail is called your permanent record. So think before you post.

clues.title: Manners in messages
clues.body: Good manners online are called etiquette. In a text, email, or chat, you use kind words, no yelling in ALL CAPS, and no mean names. You also wait your turn and read before you reply. Be polite, just like in person.

clues.title: Whose picture is it?
clues.body: The student used a drawing that another artist made. A creator owns what they make. This rule is called copyright. To be fair, you must give the creator credit by naming who made it.

clues.title: Standing up online
clues.body: A classmate was being teased online. An upstander does not join in and does not stay silent. The smart steps are: first REFUSE to share the mean post, then REPORT it on the app, then SUPPORT the person who was hurt with a kind message.

clues.title: A box that watches
clues.body: A pop-up said a site uses cookies. Cookies are small files that help a site remember you and collect data about what you do. A pop-up is not a mean message and it is not bad manners. It is just the site asking to track you.

clues.title: The classroom pet
clues.body: The class turtle is named Pixel. Pixel is 12 years old and loves eating leafy greens. This is a fun fact — but it will not help you open any lock.

locks.title: The Permanent Record
locks.q: Why can a post you made months ago still be found today?
options: Because deleting a post always erases every copy right away
Because a copy can stay online, and others may have saved or shared it
Because the internet forgets everything after one week
locks.reason: A post can leave a permanent record because copies last a long time and other people may have saved or shared them, even after you delete your own.

locks.title: Good Online Manners
locks.q: Check EVERY choice that shows good etiquette in a text, email, or chat.

items.t: Reading the message before you reply
Writing in ALL CAPS to sound louder
Closing a cookie pop-up so a site cannot track you

locks.reason: Etiquette means kind words and reading before you reply; yelling in caps and mean names are rude, and a cookie pop-up is about data, not manners.

locks.title: Fair to the Creator
locks.q: When you use a picture an artist made, the rule that protects the creator is called ______.
locks.reason: Copyright is the rule that says a creator owns what they make, so you must give them credit when you use their work.

locks.title: Be an Upstander
locks.q: A classmate is teased online. Tap the steps in the RIGHT order.
locks.reason: First REFUSE to spread the mean post, then REPORT it on the app, then SUPPORT the classmate who was hurt. Refuse, Report, Support.
